quotes.text: He told me if I carried on at the same pace I'd start bleeding out of my dick, my asshole, my eyes, my nose – fucking everywhere, man. Just bleeding out of every orifice and never stopping.
quotes.speaker: Zakk Wylde
quotes.context: Black Label Society / Ozzy guitarist Zakk Wylde, laughing as he recounts his doctor's blunt warning about his extreme drinking while on blood thinners, in a 2013 Louder/Metal Hammer interview.

source.title: Black Label Society's Zakk Wylde interview: alcohol, acoustic albums and prison gigs with Ozzy Osbourne (Louder)

quotes.text: The reason we first started strumming guitars was because we wanted women and lots of them. And their daughters.
quotes.speaker: Gene Simmons
quotes.context: KISS bassist Gene Simmons, on The Oprah Winfrey Show in 1988, dismissing the idea that rock stars pick up guitars for lofty reasons — it was about groupies.

source.title: Gene Simmons Called KISS Groupie Hookups 'More Honest' Than Real Relationships (Best Life)

quotes.text: You want to know the secret to a long marriage? Separate bathrooms. Separate televisions. And every now and then you look at each other across the room and go, 'Still here, you bastard,' and somehow that's romantic.
quotes.speaker: George Burns
quotes.context: An Evening with George Burns, live Q&A at the Shubert Theatre, Chicago, October 1985, responding to an audience question about his marriage to Gracie Allen
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — George's dry, loving-but-acerbic framing of marriage, especially around Gracie, is completely plausible and he gave dozens of similar riffs. The giveaway is 'Still here, you bastard' directed at Gracie — Burns was famously reverential about her memory after her death in 1964, and even in comedy mode he rarely if ever used a harsh term about her directly.

quotes.text: I'm in one mode right now: Entertain the shit out of people. And if people don't like it, I just don't give a fuck anymore.
quotes.speaker: Bob Saget
quotes.context: Bob Saget — the wholesome 'Full House' dad whose real-life stand-up was famously filthy — on fully embracing his profane comedy persona, in a Rolling Stone interview.

source.title: A Bob Saget Interview From the 'Rolling Stone' Vault

quotes.text: I walked offstage at Caesar's one night, soaked in sweat, and some guy in a suit goes, 'Rodney, that was transcendent.' I said, 'Pal, I just told forty minutes of dick jokes to a room that smelled like cigarettes and divorce. Transcendent my ass, get me a Scotch.'
quotes.speaker: Rodney Dangerfield
quotes.context: The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson, November 1981, backstage greenroom conversation recalled in a 1994 Comedy Central retrospective taping
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Rodney's self-deprecating no-respect cadence and the specific 'Caesar's / suited guy / Scotch' texture makes it feel like a real Dangerfield ramble. The giveaway is the phrase 'smelled like cigarettes and divorce' — a little too polished and quotable for his loose, stumbling style.

quotes.text: Darling, I've had three husbands and one of them was actually useful. You can figure out which one because he's the one I don't talk about at parties.
quotes.speaker: Tallulah Bankhead
quotes.context: The Tonight Show with Jack Paar, NBC, recorded live, January 1960, during a segment on Bankhead's Broadway return
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bankhead's notorious serial-marriage history and her theatrical viciousness toward her own romantic life make this completely plausible. The giveaway is the construction 'the one I don't talk about at parties,' which is a little too winkingly clever; her real insults tended to be louder and more nakedly savage.
